question_text: A skateboarder at the top of a ramp has 2000 J of gravitational potential energy. If friction removes 400 J of energy as the skater moves down, how much kinetic energy does the skater have at the bottom?
explanation: Using energy conservation: Initial potential energy minus energy lost to friction equals final kinetic energy. So 2000 J - 400 J = 1600 J of kinetic energy at the bottom.
